_about: Access control & governance endpoints (AccessControlPolicies, GDPRAnonymization, GDPRPII, AuditLog, Ownership).

GET /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies.description: Returns a paged list of access control policies in the organisation along with the total count. Filter by active state, creator, and free-text name; sort by any supported field.

parameters.filterActive: When set, restrict to active or inactive policies.
parameters.filterCreator: Filter by policy creator (user identifier or display name).

POST /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies.description: Creates a new access control policy from the supplied model. Returns 400 with validation details when the model is invalid, the policy contains rule actions that are not registered on the server, or the filter rules fail validation. The model id is ignored — the server assigns a fresh identifier and returns it.

GET /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies/{id}.description: Returns a single access control policy by id, with the action controls appended so the UI can render the correct input controls for each rule. Returns 404 if the policy cannot be found.

PUT /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies/{id}.description: Updates an existing access control policy from the supplied model. The path id overrides any id in the body. Returns 400 with validation details when the model fails validation or contains unsupported rule actions. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for access control policy updates.

DELETE /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies/{id.description: Deletes an access control policy. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for access control policy deletion.

POST /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies/{id}/.description: Marks a policy as active so it is included when access control is evaluated on requests. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for policy activation.
Marks a policy as inactive so it is no longer evaluated, without deleting it. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for policy deactivation.

GET /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies/action.description: Returns every access control policy rule action registered on the server, along with the configurable properties each action exposes. Used by the UI to populate the action picker when building or editing policies.

GET /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies/byproc.description: Returns the access control policies arranged in the order they will be evaluated against incoming requests.

POST /api/v1/accesscontrolpolicies/setpr.description: Sets the processing order of access control policies from the supplied list. Send the policies in the order they should be evaluated.

GET /api/v1/auditLog/{objectId}/{objectT.summary: Get audit log for an object
GET /api/v1/auditLog/{objectId}/{objectT.description: Returns the paged audit log entries recorded for a single object. Internally delegates to the bulk endpoint with a single-item array for both ids.

GET /api/v1/auditLog/getByObjectTypeIds.summary: Get audit log by object types
GET /api/v1/auditLog/getByObjectTypeIds.description: Returns paged audit log entries filtered by object ids, object types, author, and date range. When `objectTypeIds` is empty, every known object type is considered. The server restricts the requested types to those the caller has at least View permission on (Edit for some sensitive types such as export targets and role administration), so the result may be narrower than requested.

parameters.objectIds: Optional list of audited object ids.
parameters.objectTypeIds: Optional list of object type ids. When omitted, every type the caller can read is considered.
parameters.authorId: Optional filter for the user who made the change.
parameters.fromDate: Optional lower bound on entry timestamp.
parameters.toDate: Optional upper bound on entry timestamp.

GET /api/v1/identifiers.description: Returns the personally-identifiable-information (PII) identifier facets known to the organisation, used by the UI to drive PII drill-down. An optional `identifierType` query parameter narrows the result to one identifier family.

parameters.identifierType: Optional identifier type to scope the result to.

GET /api/v1/identifying.description: Returns sensitive data counts grouped by provider, computed from the metrics store. Each entry lists the provider id and name and the counts of records carrying each sensitive data classification (only sensitive classifications with non-zero counts are returned).

GET /api/v1/indentifying.description: Alias for `GET /api/v1/identifying` kept under the historical `indentifying` (misspelled) path for backwards compatibility. Same response shape and semantics.

POST /api/v1/ownership/object.description: Adds an owner to the supplied object. The caller must hold at least Consultant level on any of their claims. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for ownership additions.

DELETE /api/v1/ownership/object.description: Removes an owner from the supplied object. The caller must hold at least Consultant level on any of their claims. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for ownership removals.

GET /api/v1/ownership/object/{objectId}.description: Returns the owners registered on a specific object along with the total count.

parameters.objectId: Identifier of the object to look up.

POST /api/v1/pseudo/gdpr.description: Submits a pseudonymisation request for an entity across every provider definition the caller has write access to. For each allowed provider a `mesh` command is published to the service bus with `IsPseudo=true`; providers the caller cannot access are surfaced to the user via notifications. Returns 202 Accepted with the allowed providers, 404 if the entity does not exist, or 403 if the caller has no provider access for the entity. The reprocess command is queued so the entity is re-processed after the providers act on the request.

parameters.id: Identifier of the entity to pseudonymise.
